contents.value:
```caddyfile
example.com {
    respond /health "OK" 200
    respond /robots.txt "User-agent: *\nDisallow: /" 200
}
```
**respond** returns a static HTTP response. Commonly used for health checks, simple responses, and blocking paths. Accepts status codes and response bodies. Can set custom headers.

```caddyfile
example.com {
    redir /old-blog /new-blog permanent
    redir /go/{path} https://other.com/{path} temporary
}
```
**redir** sends an HTTP redirect response. `permanent` = 301, `temporary` = 302. Supports placeholders like `{host}`, `{uri}`, `{path}` for dynamic redirects.

```caddyfile
example.com {
    root * /var/www/html
    file_server
}
```
**root** sets the root directory for all file-related handlers (file_server, try_files, etc.). The `*` matcher means it applies to all requests. Path can use placeholders.

```caddyfile
example.com {
    root * /var/www/spa
    try_files {path} {path}/ /index.html
    file_server
}
```
**try_files** rewrites the URI to the first matching file on disk. Critical for SPAs (React, Vue, Angular) where client-side routing needs all paths to serve index.html. Also used in PHP setups to fall back to index.php.

```caddyfile
example.com {
    root * /var/www/html
    templates
    file_server
}
```
**templates** evaluates Go template actions in the response body. Templates have access to request data, environment variables, file includes, markdown rendering, and more. Files are processed as templates before being served.

```caddyfile
example.com {
    uri strip_prefix /api
    uri strip_suffix .html
    uri replace /old-path /new-path
    uri path_regexp /user/(\d+) /profile?id=$1
}
```
**uri** manipulates the request URI internally. `strip_prefix` and `strip_suffix` remove path components. `replace` does string substitution. `path_regexp` applies regex-based rewriting.

hovers.forward_auth:
## forward_auth

```caddyfile
forward_auth [match] <upstreams...> {
    uri             <path>
    copy_headers    <header_names...>
    header_up       <header> <value>
    header_down     <header> <value>
}
```

Delegates authentication to an external service. Sends a subrequest to the specified upstream and:
- On **2xx response**: copies configured headers to the original request and continues
- On **non-2xx**: returns the auth service's response to the client

**Example — Authelia/Authentik:**
```caddyfile
forward_auth localhost:9091 {
    uri /api/verify?rd=https://auth.example.com
    copy_headers Remote-User Remote-Groups Remote-Email
}
```

**Example — simple token check:**
```caddyfile
forward_auth localhost:5000 {
    uri /auth/check
    copy_headers X-User-Id X-User-Role
    header_up X-Original-URI {uri}
}
```
hovers.request_header:
## request_header

```caddyfile
request_header [match] [[+|-]field [value|regexp replacement]]
```

Manipulate incoming request headers before they reach handlers.

```caddyfile
# Set (overwrite) a header
request_header X-Real-IP {remote_host}

# Add (+) a header value
request_header +X-Forwarded-For {remote_host}

# Delete (-) a header
request_header -Proxy
request_header -X-Powered-By

# Regex replace
request_header Authorization "Bearer (.*)" "Token $1"
```
hovers.global_options:
## Global Options Block

```caddyfile
{
    # TLS & ACME
    email     admin@example.com
    acme_ca   https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
    acme_dns  cloudflare {env.CF_API_TOKEN}
    local_certs
    auto_https off|disable_redirects|ignore_loaded_certs
    on_demand_tls { ask http://localhost/check }

    # Server
    admin      off|localhost:2019
    grace_period 10s
    servers { protocols h1 h2 h3 }

    # Logging
    log { level INFO }

    # Certificate management
    pki { ca local { ... } }
    skip_install_trust
    ocsp_stapling off

    # Ordering
    order reverse_proxy before respond

    # Storage
    storage file_system /data/caddy
}
```

**Note:** The global options block must be the **first** item in the Caddyfile and has no key (just `{ }`).
hovers.matchers:
## Named Matchers

Matchers filter which requests a directive applies to.

```caddyfile
# Define named matcher
@api {
    path /api/*
    method POST PUT DELETE
}

# Use with directive
reverse_proxy @api localhost:8080

# Inline (single matcher)
reverse_proxy /api/* localhost:8080
```

**Available matchers:**
| Matcher | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `path` | URI path pattern |
| `path_regexp` | Regex path matching |
| `method` | HTTP method |
| `header` | Request header presence/value |
| `host` | Hostname |
| `remote_ip` | Client IP |
| `protocol` | HTTP/HTTPS |
| `query` | Query string parameters |
| `file` | File existence |
| `expression` | CEL expression |
| `not` | Negate a matcher |

**Combining matchers** (AND logic within one matcher, OR with multiple `@name`):
```caddyfile
@secure {
    protocol https
    path /admin/*
}
```
hovers.placeholders:
## Placeholders

Caddy provides request/response placeholders for dynamic configuration.

**Common placeholders:**
| Placeholder | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `{host}` | Request hostname |
| `{method}` | HTTP method |
| `{path}` | URI path |
| `{query}` | Query string |
| `{uri}` | Full URI (path + query) |
| `{remote_host}` | Client IP address |
| `{upstream_hostport}` | Selected upstream address |
| `{http.request.header.*}` | Request header value |
| `{http.response.header.*}` | Response header value |
| `{http.request.cookie.*}` | Cookie value |
| `{http.request.tls.version}` | TLS version |
| `{http.reverse_proxy.upstream.latency}` | Upstream response time |
| `{env.*}` | Environment variable |
| `{err.status_code}` | Error status code |
| `{err.status_text}` | Error status text |

**Shorthand ↔ Full form:**
- `{host}` = `{http.request.host}`
- `{path}` = `{http.request.uri.path}`
- `{method}` = `{http.request.method}`
hovers.on_demand_tls:
## On-Demand TLS

Obtain certificates at TLS handshake time instead of in advance.

**In global options:**
```caddyfile
{
    on_demand_tls {
        ask      http://localhost:5555/check  # verification endpoint
        interval 2m   # min time between certificate operations
        burst    5    # max simultaneous certificate operations
    }
}
```

**In site block:**
```caddyfile
https:// {
    tls {
        on_demand
    }
    reverse_proxy localhost:8080
}
```

**How it works:**
1. Client connects with SNI hostname
2. Caddy asks the `ask` endpoint if allowed
3. If endpoint returns 200, certificate is obtained
4. Any other status: TLS handshake fails

**Use case:** Multi-tenant SaaS where domains are dynamic.
hovers.site_address:
## Site Addresses

Caddy automatically manages HTTPS for sites.

```caddyfile
# Automatic HTTPS
example.com

# With explicit port
example.com:8443

# Multiple addresses
example.com, www.example.com

# HTTP only
http://example.com

# All interfaces on port
:8080

# Wildcard (needs DNS challenge)
*.example.com

# Localhost (uses internal CA)
localhost

# Network address
192.168.1.1:443
```

**Automatic HTTPS behavior:**
- Public domains → Let's Encrypt / ZeroSSL
- `localhost` / IP → Internal Caddy CA
- `http://` prefix → no HTTPS
- `*.domain` → requires DNS challenge (`acme_dns`)
- Port `:80` → no HTTPS
hovers.directive_ordering:
## Directive Ordering

Caddy processes directives in a **predefined order**, not file order.

**Default order (simplified):**
1. `tracing`
2. `map`, `vars`
3. `root`
4. `header`, `request_header`
5. `redir`
6. `rewrite`, `uri`
7. `basicauth`, `forward_auth`
8. `request_body`
9. `encode`
10. `templates`
11. `reverse_proxy`
12. `respond`
13. `abort`
14. `file_server`

**Override with `route`** (uses file order):
```caddyfile
route {
    rewrite /old /new
    reverse_proxy localhost:3000
}
```

**Override globally:**
```caddyfile
{
    order forward_auth before basicauth
    order request_header before reverse_proxy
}
```

contents.value:
```caddyfile
{
    acme_ca https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
    # Staging: https://acme-staging-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
}
```
**acme_ca** global option sets the ACME Certificate Authority URL. Default is Let's Encrypt production. Use the staging URL during testing to avoid rate limits.

```caddyfile
{
    admin off                     # disable admin API
    admin localhost:2019          # default
    admin unix//run/caddy.sock    # Unix socket
}
```
**admin** global option configures the admin API endpoint. Default: `localhost:2019`. Use `off` to disable. The admin API allows runtime config changes, certificate management, and metrics via REST.

```caddyfile
{
    auto_https off                # disable all auto HTTPS
    auto_https disable_redirects  # HTTPS but no HTTP→HTTPS redirect
    auto_https disable_certs      # redirects but no certs
    auto_https ignore_loaded_certs
}
```
**auto_https** global option controls automatic HTTPS behavior. By default, Caddy obtains certificates and redirects HTTP to HTTPS. Use this to customize or disable parts of that automation.

```caddyfile
{
    order rate_limit before basicauth
    order my_plugin after encode
}
```
**order** global option overrides the default execution order of HTTP handler directives. Each directive has a standard position; use `order` to insert custom or third-party directives at specific positions in the middleware chain.

**ocsp_stapling** global option controls OCSP stapling behavior. By default, Caddy automatically staples OCSP responses to TLS handshakes for improved client performance and privacy. Set to `off` to disable.
